feature: Vehicle Transport Platform Phase 1 - working app name configurable
version: PRD v1.6 - Feedback.pdf traceability May 2026 (prototype sync 2026-05-25)
goal: Replace manual vehicle transport dispatch with a contract-driven Version 1 platform focused on operational order creation, marketplace job distribution, direct assignment, binding driver acceptance, clear Report Problem cancellation and special case flows, external map navigation, address master data, simple push notifications for drivers, admin email alerts for critical events, optional tour-linked document and driver invoice handling, auditability, and a controlled rollout from zero without historical Excel tour migration.

product_context.platform_type: Vehicle transport / vehicle relocation operations platform
product_context.core_object: Transport order / tour / official transport-order PDF
source_inputs: Vehicle / operational CSV list (legacy column glossary)
Translated written client feedback PDF, May 2026
important_notes: Drivers transport vehicles between clearly separated pickup and delivery locations.
Each order has one customer as required reporting and billing context, but the customer must not automatically define one fixed pickup or delivery address.
Pickup and delivery locations have separate address, contact, date, and time window data.
Drivers cannot directly reject directly assigned jobs.
After acceptance, drivers cannot casually return a tour. They use Report Problem with either Cancel Order or Report Order as Not Performable.
Report Order as Not Performable creates a Special Case requiring admin decision. It must not automatically cancel the order.
Published and Assigned are separate mutually exclusive paths in Phase 1.
Dispatch may return a Published marketplace job to Draft as an admin action to edit, re-publish, or direct-assign.
Admins may cancel, republish, adjust, close, or resolve jobs where operations require it, with full audit history.
Performed means the tour was completed operationally. Document review and settlement states are tracked separately.
Version 1 uses external map app handoff for pickup and delivery navigation, not an internal map or GPS tracking module.
Drivers should not receive workflow emails from the system. Driver workflow communication should happen through the PWA and push notifications where relevant.
Admins can receive system email alerts with deep links for critical events in Version 1; a full backend notification center is future scope unless trivial in the chosen platform.

Full finance dashboards, accounting integration, vehicle condition documentation, historical Excel tour migration, CSV mass import, and complex tour chains are out of Version 1 unless separately approved.
The final app display name should be configurable because the client does not want the program to be named AUTHEON in the UI.
Draft jobs may be permanently deleted; they are not cancelled. Cancel Order in the driver PWA requires a minimum-length explanation before slide-to-confirm unlocks.

Driver profile master data (company, address, email, phone, driver ID) is maintained by admin/dispatching. Drivers do not self-edit those fields in V1; they submit change requests. At most one open request per driver until admin approves or rejects.
Cancel Order shows binding-cost warning and link to driver terms before slide-to-confirm. Not Performable may attach evidence files (photos/PDF) visible to admin on the special-case panel.
product_context.prototype_reference: prototype/project (single-page HTML/React Babel demo: admin.jsx, driver.jsx, store.js, i18n.js, inputFormatters.js — not production repo layout; maps conceptually to apps/* paths in tasks). Final production PRD v1.6 supersedes prototype-only return request and broad finance assumptions with client-validated Report Problem, Special Case, and finance de-scoping decisions.

prototype_coverage_notes: Driver PWA (demo): marketplace with pull-to-refresh, My Jobs (Active, Performed, Cancelled, Special case), locked preview, slide accept, map handoff, Mark as performed, Report Problem (seven cancel reasons; cancel path with binding warning + driver terms link; warning icon on tour footer and sheet header; not performable with optional evidence upload up to 5 files), tour-linked document upload only after Performed (categories, rejection reason, replace file), correction badge on Performed tab only, Infopoint (General documents with description, date, view/download stub; New messages with read/unread badge), profile with read-only master data, change-request form (blocked while open request pending), push preferences (no driver workflow email).

Admin (demo): job overview with special case and performed filters, primary New job CTA on Jobs overview (no separate New job sidebar item), job detail with draft delete and special case resolution (Continue, Republish, administrative close, cancel order) including not-performable evidence file list, distance estimate/recalculate, new order input formatters (date/time blur, VIN/plate normalize, manufacturer datalist, schedule/time-window warnings via inputFormatters.js), full CRUD for customers (all PRD fields, active flag, delete blocked when referenced by tours) and addresses (full location/contact fields, active flag, usage count), new order form with customer select plus pickup/delivery selects from address master data and optional save-to-master checkboxes for manual entry, direct assign and reassign via driver picker (active drivers only), driver invoices and documents with review/reject/replace/bulk download (status via pill only, no full-row green highlight), Infopoint admin (General documents: upload stub, add/rename/replace/hide; New messages: publish form with subject, body, date, optional in-app/push notify), Profile change requests nav (open/approved/rejected filters, inline driver edit, Approve & save applies updateDriver then closes request; Reject closes without save), Users pane driver edit, audit log, admin notification feed with Review request deep-link, admin email alert demo, CSV export. Finance dashboard removed.

Driver Report Problem (demo): Cancel Order uses slide-to-confirm locked until explanation has at least 10 characters, with clear disabled visual state; Not Performable uses Submit with optional file attachments stored on specialCaseReport.evidence.

Publishing (demo): admin revert Published to Draft; publish and direct assign from Draft only (assign opens modal to pick active driver); admin can reassign driver on Assigned, Accepted, or Special case tours; admin can delete Draft jobs from job detail.
Status model (demo): operational statuses draft, published, accepted, assigned, performed, cancelled, special_case only — no return_requested or completed.
Branding (demo): configurable display name default Transport Portal; AUTHEON not shown in UI chrome.
i18n: EN/DE for UI strings; audit log action keys in English.

Seed data: 10 jobs, 5 customers, 17 addresses, 3 drivers (2 active, 1 blocked), 6 tour-linked seed documents (only on performed tours; rejected invoice on performed 0842, not on active tours), sample admin email alerts, expanded audit log; validateSeedData() on load checks contacts, addresses, schedules, vehicle IDs, driver offer, history, driverState consistency, performed-tour documents, and no documents on non-performed jobs.

prototype_validation.verdict: PASS — prototype matches PRD v1.6 at docs/requirements/prd.json (_audit-prototype.mjs exit 0; checklist items 1–28 in prd-prototype-validation.md).
sync_pass_notes: Integrated client feedback requiring customer, pickup location, and delivery location separation.
Replaced return window logic with Report Problem, cancellation, and special case handling.
De-scoped full Finance dashboard, vehicle condition documentation, CSV mass import, historical migration, internal map module, and complex tour chains from base V1.
Added address master data, pull to refresh, external map navigation, admin email alerts, Infopoint, driver management, and rollout-from-zero assumptions.

2026-05-21: Admin draft permanent delete; Jobs overview primary New job entry (removed duplicate sidebar nav item); Cancel Order slide gated on min. 10-character explanation with disabled slider styling; special case resolution actions documented as Continue, Republish, administrative close (Performed + settlement Closed), and Cancel order.
2026-05-21: Prototype seed jobs and address master data completed with pickup/delivery contacts (person, phone, email, second phone) on every tour.

2026-05-21: New job form customer is a required select from customer master data; tour documents only after Performed; driver/admin document replace on performed tours; correction badge only on performed My Jobs.

2026-05-21: Direct assign from Draft and reassign on Assigned/Accepted/Special case via driver picker (active drivers only); driver invoices table uses status pills without persistent row green background.
2026-05-21: Special case Continue restores statusBeforeSpecialCase (e.g. Accepted) instead of always Assigned; driverState.acceptedIds resynced on continue.
2026-05-21: Customer and address master data CRUD in prototype (create/edit/delete/deactivate, usage guards, job form integration).
2026-05-21: New job form and displays use separate pickup and delivery dates/time windows (multi-day tours supported).
2026-05-21: Admin Driver invoices nav renamed Tour documents with type/review/source filters and document-type column; off-channel register supports all tour document types.
2026-05-21: Canonical tour billing invoice document type is `invoice` only; the legacy billing-invoice code was removed and normalized in prototype store.
2026-05-21: Tour document review uses uploaded, accepted, rejected, and correction_required; admin Require correction promotes rejected → correction_required for driver workflow.
2026-05-21: paymentStatus defaults to Invoice Missing; accepting tour invoice sets Invoice Received; Paid only via Finance manual edit.
2026-05-23 v1.6 spec: Added client_feedback_traceability, driver_visibility_matrix, notification_channels_matrix, marketplace_sync_policy, client_status_mapping in prd.json.
2026-05-23 v1.6 spec: Alternate contact fields, admin notification feed, auto Under Review on first post-Performed upload, three push toggles, push_on_direct_assign false.
2026-05-25: Consolidated prd_updated_v2.json into prd.json; AUTHEON/prd.json is the only canonical spec file.
2026-05-25: Admin Infopoint pane (general documents + news publish); driver Infopoint doc view/download stub; clarifies Infopoint news vs tour notifications.

2026-05-25: Client feedback gap pass — Report Problem cancel binding warning + terms; seven cancellation reason codes; not-performable evidence on specialCaseReport; Report Problem warning icon; order-entry formatters (inputFormatters.js).

2026-05-25: Driver profile change requests — persistent masterDataChangeRequests queue; one open request per driver; admin Profile change requests pane with inline edit and Approve & save; driver pending state; notification feed Review request link; driver notifications on approve/reject.
2026-06-29: Driver deactivation guard — admin cannot block, deactivate, archive, or soft-delete a driver while assigned/accepted/special_case tours are open; activation remains allowed.

job_fields: axle type: driven on own wheels / third-party axle
billing_invoice_document_fields: original file name and MIME type
source driver PWA or admin off-channel
master_data_change_request_fields: snapshot at submit: company, address, email, phone
special_case_evidence_fields: stored on specialCaseReport.evidence for not-performable (prototype: metadata only, no blob storage)

global_business_rules: A job can follow either marketplace flow or direct assignment flow.
Marketplace flow: Draft → Published → Accepted → Performed. Document review and settlement are tracked separately after or alongside Performed.
Direct assignment flow: Draft → Assigned → Performed. Document review and settlement are tracked separately after or alongside Performed.
Published → Assigned is not part of Phase 1. If a published job must be changed or reassigned manually, it should return to Draft first through an admin action.
Admin may revert Published → Draft directly. This removes the job from the marketplace and enables edit, re-publish, or direct assignment.

Admin may permanently delete Draft jobs that were created in error or are no longer needed. Deletion is allowed only while status is Draft; published or committed jobs use cancel, revert, or special-case flows instead.
Driver acceptance must be atomic. Only one driver can successfully accept a Published job.
After acceptance or direct assignment, sensitive job details become visible only to the assigned or accepting driver.

Before acceptance, marketplace previews show only reduced data such as rough pickup/delivery region, distance, relevant dates/time windows, vehicle type, axle type, and driver offer and customer price where applicable.
Directly assigned jobs appear in the assigned driver's My Jobs area and do not appear in the marketplace.
Admin may reassign a different active driver on tours in Assigned, Accepted, or Special Case without changing operational status; reassignment is audited and notifies the tour context.
Published tours cannot be directly reassigned without returning to Draft first.
Assigned jobs cannot be rejected directly by the driver.
After acceptance, a driver cannot casually return a tour. The driver must use Report Problem.
Report Problem has two main driver paths: Cancel Order and Report Order as Not Performable.
Cancel Order records actor, timestamp, reason, optional free text, optional evidence, and triggers an admin alert.
Report Order as Not Performable creates Special Case status. Special Case does not automatically cancel the order.
Admins decide whether a Special Case should continue, be adjusted, be republished, be administratively closed, be billed separately, or be cancelled.
Special case administrative close means operational status Performed with settlement or administrative closure marked Closed — it is not the same as cancelling the order.
Special case cancel order transitions the job to Cancelled and ends the tour operationally.
Admin creates new jobs from the Jobs overview primary action; Version 1 does not use a separate persistent sidebar navigation item for New job.

Admins may transition Accepted, Assigned, Performed, or Special Case jobs to Cancelled where operations require it and permissions allow; administrative closure should be tracked through settlement/document closure state rather than treated as normal operational completion.
Performed means the vehicle transfer was completed operationally. It does not mean all documents, invoices, receipts, or settlement steps are complete.
Document review status is separate from operational job status. Documents can be Missing, Uploaded, Under Review, Accepted, Rejected, or Correction Required.
Settlement or invoice processing state is separate from operational job status and document review status if included in scope.
PDF generation must be based on the current structured job data.
Only the currently active PDF needs to be visible in the base scope; regeneration events must be auditable.
Tour-linked documents and driver invoice files are always submitted in the context of a specific job/tour. There is no driver-facing unattached invoice inbox in the PWA.
Rejected documents must create a visible correction need for the driver and include a rejection reason.
Drivers should not receive workflow emails from the system. Driver workflow communication should happen via PWA views and push notifications where relevant.

Admin critical alerts can be sent as system emails with deep links to the relevant order in Version 1. The audit log remains the traceability record but is not enough by itself for urgent operational events.
Postal code and country are stored separately. The postal code remains in the native country format.
The system must preserve manual admin control for edge cases and must not block operations merely because an exceptional workflow was not predicted.
Version 1 supports simple A to B tours only. A to B to A and A to B to C tours are represented as separate orders for now.
Rollout starts from zero. Historical Excel tours are not migrated into the new system.
Administrative Closed state means the order is fully checked, documented, and financially or administratively closed; it is separate from operational Performed status.
Driver visibility before and after acceptance follows driver_visibility_matrix; server-side authorization enforces the matrix.
On first tour-linked document upload after operational Performed, job document_review_summary transitions to Under Review and triggers admin notification per notification_channels_matrix.
Admin lightweight notification feed lists critical operational events in the admin UI; email alerts complement the feed for urgent events.
Driver in-app notifications inform drivers of tour changes, document review outcomes, special case resolution, and new Infopoint messages without workflow email.
Cancellation attribution records whether cancellation came from driver, admin/dispatching, or customer (client).
Status history for every operational transition records previous status, new status, date/time, triggering user, optional comment, and uploaded evidence reference when applicable.
Pickup and delivery optional contact blocks use progressive disclosure (Add another contact / Show additional contact details) so the default form stays lean.
Admin may update linked customer or address master data from order entry when business allows; changes are audited and do not silently rewrite historical PDFs unless regenerated.
Marketplace synchronization in Version 1 uses load-on-open and pull-to-refresh per marketplace_sync_policy; continuous real-time list mutation is not required.
Push notifications for newly published jobs require driver opt-in via three toggles; admin pre-activation is not required. Direct assign does not trigger marketplace-style new-job push by default.
Admin may mark a document as checked before final accept/reject when document review workflow requires a distinct checked step.
UI may display client-friendly labels Under review and Completed derived from document_review_summary and settlement state without adding operational Completed status.

tasks.story: As a platform owner, I want a persisted domain model for orders/tours, customers, reusable addresses, drivers, documents, statuses, and basic financial/settlement fields so all workflows share one source of truth.
acceptance: Job entity clearly separates customer, pickup location, and delivery location.
Job entity includes separate pickup date, pickup time window from/to, delivery date, and delivery time window from/to.
Job entity stores postal code and country separately for pickup and delivery; country is not encoded into the postal code string.
Job entity includes contact person, phone, optional second phone, optional email, driver-visible notes, and internal notes for pickup and delivery where applicable.
Customer is a required reporting and billing context but must not automatically define one fixed pickup or delivery address.
Address master data entity supports reusable address records that can be used as pickup or delivery locations.
Address records support name, street, house number, postal code, city, country, contact person, phone, optional second phone, optional email, notes, and active/archive metadata.

Job entity includes vehicle data, VIN, license plate, axle type using English labels such as driven on own wheels / third-party axle, distance estimate, manual distance override, pricing inputs where operationally needed, and generated PDF reference.
Operational job status is separated from document review status and settlement state.
Operational statuses include Draft, Published, Accepted, Assigned, Performed, Cancelled, and Special Case.
Document review statuses include Missing, Uploaded, Under Review, Accepted, Rejected, and Correction Required.
Settlement or invoice processing states include Not Started, Pending, Processed, Paid, Needs Clarification, and Closed if included in scope.
Driver/driver entity includes identity, company/person contact information, status, driver identifier, access state, and notification/filter preferences.
Schema/migration artifacts are versioned.

tasks.story: As an admin or driver, I want secure role-based access so every user only sees and changes allowed data while driver access stays simple.
acceptance: Keycloak is the identity provider and source of truth for application roles/groups.
Production application roles are admin and driver; generic public self-registration is not part of Version 1.
Each driver has protected individual access because invoices and documents may be uploaded.
AUTHEON stores a local app user profile linked to the Keycloak subject; it does not store passwords or duplicate role-assignment tables.

Production driver and admin account provisioning uses Keycloak action emails/invite links for initial password setup and password reset; AUTHEON admins never view, copy, store, or manually send generated passwords.

The backend creates or links the Keycloak user, assigns the correct role/group, persists the local app user/profile link, and then requests Keycloak or the configured mail provider to send the invite/reset action.
Invite and reset links expire according to Keycloak realm policy and can be resent by an authorized admin.
Exact driver login ceremony is configured in Keycloak and should keep first login simple while still forcing the user to set their own password.
Admin can create admin and driver accounts and can resend or reset driver access.
Admin can manage orders, customers, addresses, drivers, documents, driver invoices, Infopoint content, audit logs, and status decisions according to permissions.

Driver can access only Published jobs in the marketplace, own Assigned/Accepted/Performed/Cancelled/Special Case jobs where allowed, profile, Information Center/Infopoint, and permitted PDFs/documents.
Blocked or inactive drivers cannot access marketplace active actions or accept new tours.
Archived or soft-deleted drivers remain linked to historical tours for traceability according to data retention rules.
Unauthorized API calls return safe error messages and never leak sensitive data.

tasks.story: As an admin, I want to create and manage drivers, access, and backend users so platform access is controlled and historical links remain preserved.
acceptance: Admin can create and edit driver records.
Creating a driver account in production creates or links a local app user, a driver profile, and a Keycloak user in one auditable backend workflow.
Driver account creation requires email, person/contact name, company name, and driver ID; the driver profile remains separate from the identity account but is linked through userId/keycloak subject.
After driver creation, the system sends an invitation or required-action email for the driver to set their own password; the admin UI shows delivery status and resend action, not the password.
Driver record includes company name, person name, contact data, driver ID, notes, account/access state, and operational status.
Driver statuses include Active, Blocked, Inactive, Archived, and Soft Deleted.
Blocked or inactive drivers cannot accept new tours or use active marketplace actions.
Admin cannot block, deactivate, archive, or soft-delete a driver while that driver has active tours (assigned, accepted, or special case); activation is always allowed.
Historical tours remain linked to archived or soft-deleted drivers.
Hard delete is avoided except for test records or records with no historical activity, subject to compliance rules.
Admin can reset, resend, or regenerate driver access.
Admin can create, edit, activate, and deactivate backend/admin accounts.
Every activation, block, inactive/archive/soft-delete, access reset, or Keycloak role/group change consumed by AUTHEON is logged where available.
Admin can reset or resend access for backend/admin users as well as drivers, subject to role permissions and audit logging.

Admin can edit all driver master data fields including company name, person name, contact data, driver ID, notes, status, and access state; changes to status, block, archive, or access reset are audited.
Drivers may submit a master-data change request with a minimum-length note when profile fields must change; they cannot edit master data fields directly in the driver PWA.
At most one open master-data change request per driver; additional submissions are blocked until admin approves or rejects the open request.
Admin has a dedicated queue to list and filter requests (open, approved, rejected) with driver note and snapshot of profile data at request time.
Admin resolves requests by Approve & save (apply driver field edits from the resolution form, then close as approved) or Reject (close without applying edits).
master_data_change_requested appears in the admin notification feed; admin can navigate to the request queue from the feed.

tasks.story: As an admin, I want reusable customer and shared address master data so order creation is fast without forcing every address to belong to one customer.
acceptance: Admin can create and edit customer records for reporting, billing context, analysis, and monitoring.
Admin can create and edit shared address records independently from customers.
The same address record can be used as pickup location in one order and delivery location in another order.
Address records contain name, street, house number, postal code, city, country, contact person, phone, optional second phone, optional email, and notes.
A single Name field can represent either a company or a private person; no mandatory person/company type split is required for V1.
Order creation can search or autocomplete saved addresses for pickup and delivery.
Admin can enter a free address without saving it.
Admin can choose whether to save a newly entered address as master data.
One-time private addresses should not automatically pollute the address master data list.
Master data changes do not unintentionally rewrite historical job PDFs unless explicitly regenerated.
Order entry must not be blocked if a saved address does not exist.
Admin can update an existing customer or address master data record from order entry when permitted, with audit logging.
Updating master data from order entry does not silently change text on already-generated historical PDFs unless admin explicitly regenerates the PDF.

tasks.story: As an admin, I want to create and save orders as drafts with structured sections for customer, pickup, delivery, time windows, vehicle data, distance, and notes.
acceptance: Creation form includes clear sections: Customer, Pickup Location, Delivery Location, Pickup Time Window, Delivery Time Window, Vehicle, Distance, Driver offer and customer price inputs if in scope, Driver Notes, and Internal Notes.
Customer is required for reporting and billing context and must be chosen from customer master data via select control (not unstructured free-text entry).
Selecting a customer sets customerId and display name and may prefill default contact fields where master data provides them.
Pickup and delivery locations are independent sections and can be selected from shared address master data or entered freely.
Pickup and delivery each have separate date, time window from, and time window to fields.

System validates obvious date/time inconsistencies such as delivery date before pickup date or time window to before time window from; admins may consciously confirm warnings where business requires flexibility.
Date input supports free text and calendar picker and formats to DD.MM.YYYY.
Time input supports free text and formats values such as 0800 to 08:00 and 830 to 08:30.
Vehicle manufacturer supports dropdown suggestions plus free text; unknown manufacturers are not blocked.
Vehicle model supports free text and optional suggestions.

VIN input allows up to 17 characters, uppercases letters, removes spaces, avoids invalid special characters, and shows a notice if fewer than 17 characters are entered; saving without VIN remains possible with warning.
License plate input supports free text, uppercase normalization, removal of unnecessary spaces, sensible formatting, and German or foreign plates without overly rigid German-only validation.
Job can be saved as Draft.
System generates unique tour number using agreed format such as 0001-26, with yearly sequencing.
Required fields are validated before Publish or Direct Assign.
Sensitive fields are persisted but not exposed to drivers before acceptance or assignment.
Pickup and delivery sections support optional alternate contact person distinct from primary contact person.
Optional contact fields (alternate contact, second phone, email, info notes) are hidden behind progressive disclosure such as Add another contact or Show additional contact details.

tasks.story: As an admin, I want to publish jobs to the marketplace or assign them directly so jobs follow the correct operational path.
acceptance: Publish transitions Draft to Published only after validation.

Direct assign transitions Draft to Assigned only after selecting an eligible active driver through an assign dialog (dropdown or equivalent); blocked, inactive, archived, or soft-deleted drivers are not selectable.

Admin can reassign a different active driver on tours in Assigned, Accepted, or Special Case; reassignment updates driver id and display name, preserves operational status, records status history and audit log, and must not allow selecting the already-assigned driver.
Reassign is blocked for Draft, Published, Performed, and Cancelled unless business rules route through revert or special-case flows first.
Assigned jobs do not appear in the marketplace.
Published jobs appear only as reduced previews in the marketplace.
Published to Assigned without Draft is blocked in Phase 1 unless the job is returned to Draft first.
Admin can revert Published to Draft, which removes the job from marketplace and enables edit, re-publish, or direct assignment.
Revert is available only for Published jobs, not as a replacement for accepted-job cancellation/problem handling.
Publish, direct assignment, reassignment, and revert-to-draft actions create audit log entries.
Version 1 supports simple A to B tours only; A to B to A and A to B to C tour chains are represented as separate orders for now.

tasks.story: As a driver, I want to browse available published jobs with filters and refresh behavior so I can find relevant transports.
acceptance: Marketplace displays only jobs with status Published.

Job cards show reduced data: rough pickup region/postal code, rough delivery region/postal code, distance, relevant date/time window, vehicle type, axle type, and driver offer and customer price where applicable.
Before acceptance, full addresses, contact persons, phone numbers, VIN, license plate, internal notes, and sensitive instructions are not returned to the driver client.
Filters include postal code or location, date or date range, vehicle type, and driven on own wheels versus third-party axle where applicable.
German postal code prefix filtering supports at least one-digit and two-digit prefix filters such as 4, 40, or 41.
Foreign or unknown postal codes must not crash filtering or incorrectly hide jobs.
Cross-border country filtering is future scope.
Marketplace loads current data when opened.
Marketplace supports pull to refresh while active.
Full real-time mutation of the visible list while the user is watching is not required for Version 1.
Prototype auto reload behavior came from mocked backend behavior and must not be treated as final production logic.
Marketplace supports empty state when no matching jobs exist.
Blocked or inactive drivers cannot access marketplace active actions.
Data leakage tests verify hidden fields are not returned before acceptance.
Publishing a job makes it visible to authorized drivers on the next marketplace fetch (open screen or pull-to-refresh), not via mandatory real-time WebSocket sync in Version 1.
Behavior aligns with marketplace_sync_policy answers: newly published orders appear after fetch; pull-to-refresh is the manual refresh path.

tasks.story: As a driver, I want job details to show safe information before acceptance and full operational information plus navigation after commitment.
acceptance: Before acceptance, visible fields are limited to route summary, distance, relevant time windows, vehicle type, axle type, and driver offer and customer price where applicable.
Before acceptance, customer, full addresses, contacts, phone numbers, VIN, license plate, internal notes, and sensitive instructions are hidden.
Locked section clearly explains that details unlock after acceptance.
After acceptance or direct assignment, assigned driver can see full pickup and delivery addresses, contacts, phone numbers, vehicle identifiers, notes, customer context, and permitted PDF.
After acceptance or direct assignment, driver can start navigation to pickup location from the tour detail view.
After acceptance or direct assignment, driver can start navigation to delivery location from the tour detail view.
Navigation uses external map apps such as Apple Maps, Google Maps, or the device default navigation app through deep links or standard map URLs.
No internal map module is built in Version 1.
Installed map apps and device default settings remain the driver device responsibility.
Address handoff is sufficient for V1 unless route handoff is easy and low risk.
Server-side authorization enforces visibility rules.
Visibility rules implement driver_visibility_matrix including customer hidden before acceptance and visible after acceptance or direct assignment.
Before acceptance, at least pickup and delivery dates relevant to the driver decision are visible in marketplace preview.

tasks.story: As a driver, I want to accept a marketplace job with a binding confirmation so the commitment is deliberate.
acceptance: Acceptance requires slide-to-confirm or equivalent deliberate gesture.
Acceptance screen shows binding warning and links or mentions applicable terms and conditions.
Backend validates driver is active and job is still Published.
Acceptance uses atomic database logic so exactly one driver can win.
Success transitions Published to Accepted and assigns the accepting driver.
Success removes job from marketplace for other drivers.
After acceptance, the driver cannot casually return the tour and must use Report Problem for cancellation or not performable cases.
Failure state shows a clear message such as job already taken.

tasks.story: As a driver, I want to manage my assigned, accepted, performed, cancelled, and special-case tours in My Jobs.
acceptance: My Jobs includes Active and Performed/History sections or tabs.
Active includes Assigned and Accepted jobs.
Performed jobs move to a Performed or History area after the driver reports operational completion.
Cancelled jobs are either shown in a separate tab or clearly labelled historical section if required by final UX.
Special Case jobs remain visible to the assigned driver with clear status and next-step messaging until admin resolves them.
Jobs requiring document correction show a correction indicator on Performed tours only, not on Active (assigned/accepted) tours.
Each job card shows route summary, date/time, vehicle type, driver offer and customer price where applicable, and status badge.
Driver sees only own assigned, accepted, performed, cancelled, or special-case jobs.
Driver in-app notification or badge surfaces document rejection, admin cancellation, and special case resolution on affected tours in My Jobs.

tasks.story: As a driver, I want to mark a tour as performed when the vehicle transfer is operationally done.
acceptance: Completion action is available only for the driver's active Assigned or Accepted jobs.
On completion, operational status changes to Performed.
Performed means the vehicle transfer was completed operationally and does not imply documents, invoices, receipts, or settlement are complete.
Performed job moves to Performed/History area.
Completion timestamp is stored.
Completion action is disabled after operational status becomes Performed or Cancelled; administrative Closed state is tracked separately from the operational completion action.
Completion event is logged.
Tour-linked document upload and replace are available only after operational status is Performed if the document module is included; upload is blocked on Active tours with clear messaging.
Administrative or settlement closure after document/invoice review must not be confused with the driver's operational Performed action.

tasks.story: As a driver with an active tour, I want to report cancellation or not-performable problems so dispatch can decide without relying on the old return-window logic.
acceptance: Old time-dependent return window logic is replaced by Report Problem.
After acceptance, the driver cannot casually return a tour.
Report Problem provides two main paths: Cancel Order and Report Order as Not Performable.

Cancel Order requires clear confirmation such as slide-to-confirm and warning that cancellation after acceptance may trigger costs or further consequences according to applicable terms and conditions.
Cancel Order slide-to-confirm stays visually disabled until the driver enters at least 10 characters in the required explanation field; character count shows progress (e.g. x/10).
Report Order as Not Performable uses a standard Submit action after reason and explanation; it does not use the cancel slide control.
Cancellation records actor, timestamp, cancellation type, selected reason, optional free text, and optional evidence.
Cancellation reasons include driver unavailable, vehicle not available, customer cancelled order, appointment no longer possible, incorrect order data, vehicle not roadworthy, and other reason.
Admin receives an alert when a driver cancellation is submitted.

Report Order as Not Performable requires reason selection, optional description, and optional evidence upload such as photos, waiting time evidence, drop-off receipt, delivery note, or other documents.
Report Order as Not Performable transitions the job to Special Case.
Special Case does not automatically cancel the job.
Admin decides whether the job continues, is adjusted, is republished, is billed separately, is closed, or is cancelled.
Driver-facing copy reinforces immediate reporting of delays, accidents, anomalies, cancellation, or not-performable conditions to dispatch.
Driver cannot directly reject assigned jobs.

Report Order as Not Performable reason examples include vehicle not on site, vehicle not roadworthy, vehicle deregistered, red license plates required, contact person not reachable, wrong address, waiting time, empty trip/deadhead trip, damage to the vehicle, missing documents, vehicle data mismatch, and other clarification required.
Report Problem should have a clear label and icon treatment in the tour detail UI so drivers can find it quickly.

tasks.story: As an admin, I want to review cancellations and special cases with notes and evidence so operations stay controlled.
acceptance: Admin can view Report Problem submissions including job, driver, selected path, reason, free text, evidence file metadata (photos, receipts, PDFs), timestamp, and current status.
Admin can accept or finalize a driver cancellation.
Admin can republish a cancelled or special-case tour where business rules allow it.
Admin can adjust job data and continue the tour where business rules allow it.

Admin can resolve a special case with distinct outcomes: Continue (restore operational status to statusBeforeSpecialCase stored when the driver reported not performable — typically Assigned or Accepted, not always Assigned), Republish (return to Published marketplace), Administrative close (Performed with settlement/administrative Closed — case finished without treating as a driver cancellation), or Cancel order (Cancelled).
UI labels must distinguish administrative close from cancel order and from dismissing a dialog (production copy should not use ambiguous Close alone for administrative close).
Admin can add decision notes.
Decision is reflected in status history and audit log.
Special case resolution must preserve manual control and must not block exceptional operational decisions.

tasks.story: As an admin, I want to cancel jobs when operations require it and keep clear cancellation attribution.
acceptance: Admin can transition Published, Accepted, Assigned, Performed, or Special Case jobs to Cancelled where business rules allow.
Draft jobs are permanently deleted rather than cancelled; cancelled status applies only after a job has left Draft.
Backend records whether cancellation came from driver, admin/dispatching, or customer.
Cancellation requires reason and optional note.
Cancelled jobs are excluded from active marketplace and active driver job lists.
Cancellation can trigger admin/driver alerts where relevant.
Admin may republish or adjust a driver cancellation if the operational process requires it and status rules allow it.
Cancellation is recorded in status history and audit log.

tasks.story: As an admin, I want a job overview table for daily dispatch monitoring with clear operational, document, and settlement indicators.
acceptance: Table includes customer, pickup/delivery route, driver, operational status, document review summary, vehicle, date/time windows, distance, and financial/payment indicators only where feasible and in scope.
Table distinguishes operational status from document review status and settlement state.
Table supports filtering and sorting by operational status, date, driver, customer, pickup/delivery postal code, and special case state at minimum.
Critical events such as cancellation, problem case, and not-performable order are visually highlighted or accessible through admin alerts.
Export or column mapping can align with legacy spreadsheet headers where Phase 1 needs continuity, but CSV mass import is not part of Version 1.
Desktop list remains usable at high volume via pagination, density controls, or equivalent.
Rows navigate to job detail.
Drafts are visible to admins with created/updated metadata.
High-volume list remains usable on desktop.
Jobs overview provides the primary New job action; admin navigation does not include a separate always-visible New job sidebar item.

tasks.story: As an admin, I want job detail with full operational data, assignment, status history, PDF, document review state, special cases, and basic financial fields if in scope.
acceptance: Shows customer, pickup location, delivery location, contacts, time windows, vehicle data, distance estimate, manual distance override, pricing inputs where in scope, driver, notes, and document data.
Shows operational status, document review summary, settlement state if included, and status history timeline separately.
Shows generated PDF and regeneration action where allowed.
Shows Report Problem, cancellation, and Special Case state if present.
Shows uploaded documents and driver invoices linked to the tour if document/invoice module is included.
Shows basic financial fields only where operationally needed; full Finance dashboard is not base V1 unless separately confirmed.
Operational auxiliary fields from legacy spreadsheet may be supported or stubbed for export continuity, but historical Excel tour migration is out of scope.
Admin can edit Draft jobs and permitted non-critical fields according to final business rules.
Admin can delete Draft jobs permanently (hard delete or equivalent soft-delete hidden from operational lists); deletion is blocked once the job leaves Draft.
Published jobs show Return to Draft in admin actions; after revert, Draft actions such as edit, publish, and assign apply.
Draft and detail footers expose Assign driver; Assigned, Accepted, and Special case expose Reassign driver; both open the same driver picker pattern.
Assigned driver card shows name and driver id when set.
Admin can manually override distance and optionally recalculate distance if pickup or delivery addresses change.

tasks.story: As the system, I want to generate a transport-order PDF so drivers have official job instructions based on structured order data.
acceptance: PDF layout follows the provided draft Word template structure on disk, with legal sign-off before production.

PDF includes tour number, category, issue date, driver, customer context where appropriate, vehicle transfer description, distance, net price and VAT/tax totals where applicable, general instructions, protocol submission details, vehicle type, license plate, VIN, pickup and delivery sections with dates, time windows, contacts, phones, country-aware addresses, and additional instructions.
PDF uses current structured job data and English domain fields internally; final legal/customer-facing wording remains subject to review.

Operational/legal clauses align with template where required: pre-trip inspection, fuel rules, direct route, digital proof with fallback, incident reporting, safety clothing on logistics yards, weather-appropriate tires, invoice submission SLA if applicable, and binding effect after confirmation.
PDF can be generated after direct assignment and after marketplace acceptance.
If generated at publish time, driver access remains restricted until acceptance.
Admin can manually regenerate PDF after relevant job data changes.
Only current active PDF is shown in base scope.
PDF generation and regeneration are audited.
Legal wording must be reviewed before production.

tasks.story: As a driver, I want an Information Center, client-facing label Infopoint, with permanent documents and short news so I can access instructions and updates anytime.
acceptance: Info menu is renamed to Information Center / Infopoint according to final product label decision.
The area contains two main sections: General Documents and News.

General Documents contains permanent PDFs and information such as vehicle pickup instructions, vehicle drop-off instructions, behavior in case of damage, behavior in case of waiting time, fuel receipt notes, invoicing notes, driver terms and conditions, data protection information, cancellation/problem case process, and emergency contacts.
Admin can upload, rename, replace, delete, hide/show, categorize, and manage general documents.
Documents can be viewed, opened, or downloaded from the PWA.
News is one-way communication from admins to drivers.
News supports subject, text, publication date, read/unread state, and badge indicators.
News does not support replies, attachments, or rich newsletter formatting in Version 1.
Important news may optionally trigger push notification if push is implemented.
The UI should use tabs or segmented controls instead of a cluttered slider if appropriate for mobile usability.
Driver navigation label uses Infopoint; tabs or segmented controls label General documents and New messages.
Unread Infopoint news shows a badge on the Infopoint menu item and New marking on unread messages; newest messages sort first.

tasks.story: As a driver, I want to view my profile and manage simple notification preferences.
acceptance: Profile shows read-only name, company, driver ID, address, email, phone/mobile, and account status where applicable.
Profile explains that master-data changes must be requested through the operator/admin and cannot be edited directly in the PWA.
Drivers can enable or disable push notifications without requiring prior admin activation.
Notification preferences include desired pickup postal code prefix area and may include vehicle type and driven on own wheels versus third-party axle filters where in scope.
Postal code preferences support at least one-digit and two-digit German postal code prefixes such as 4, 40, or 41.
Foreign or unknown postal codes must not crash filtering or incorrectly hide jobs.
If no filters are configured, targeted notifications follow the agreed default rule.
Feature toggles may control optional driver UI visibility during rollout; toggles are auditable.

Push settings use three explicit toggle switches: master enable/disable push notifications, notification for newly published orders, and notification for new orders in desired pickup postal code prefix area (1- or 2-digit German prefix such as 4, 40, or 41).
Driver enables push without prior admin activation.

tasks.epic: Driver Push Notifications & Admin Critical Alerts
tasks.story: As a driver or admin, I want relevant notifications without overbuilding communication flows in Version 1.
acceptance: Drivers should not receive workflow emails from the system for new jobs or app events.
Driver push notifications can be triggered for newly Published jobs matching enabled preferences.

Driver push notifications can be triggered for selected important driver events if technically implemented, such as document rejection, order cancelled by admin, problem case processed, or new Information Center/Infopoint message.
Push settings are simple in Version 1: enable/disable, newly published jobs, and preferred postal code prefix area.
Matching uses driver preferences such as postal code prefix and optionally vehicle type or axle type if in scope.
Notifications are not sent for directly assigned jobs unless separately confirmed by client.
Admin audit log remains useful but is not enough for urgent operational events.
Admin critical alerts in Version 1 can be sent as system emails with deep links to the relevant order.
Critical admin events include order accepted, cancellation, problem case, not-performable order, uploaded invoice, uploaded receipt/evidence, rejected document follow-up, and performed tour.
Full backend notification center is future scope unless the selected backend platform makes it trivial.
Android support is validated.
iOS support is documented as requiring compatible iOS version, home-screen installation, and user permission.
Notification sending is logged or traceable.
Admin critical alerts may include master data change requested if a driver profile or master-data change request flow is implemented.
Version 1 includes a lightweight admin notification feed in the admin UI listing critical events from notification_channels_matrix; full backend notification center remains future scope.
Driver in-app notifications cover order cancelled by Autheon, document accepted or rejected with reason, special case processed, and new Infopoint message per notification_channels_matrix.
Admin alert when invoice, receipt, or evidence is uploaded again after initial upload (re-upload or replace).
Push notifications for newly published marketplace jobs follow driver toggles; push for direct assignment is off by default in Version 1.

tasks.epic: Basic Financial Fields & Finance Scope Control
tasks.story: As an admin, I want only the basic operational financial fields needed for dispatch and driver invoice handling while full finance dashboards remain out of Version 1 unless confirmed.
acceptance: Basic job financial fields may include revenue/price, driver offer, expenses, invoice received marker, invoice type, invoice number, payment status, and notes only where operationally useful.
Full Finance dashboard and advanced finance analytics are not validated for base Version 1 and should be future scope or needs clarification.
Driver invoice workflow remains separate from a full Finance dashboard.
Job detail may show a read-only financial snapshot if useful, but primary document/invoice actions belong to Driver Invoices/Documents if included.
No direct accounting system integration is required in Version 1.
External bookkeeping upload and payment remain manual.
Advanced margin automation and accounting integration are out of scope unless separately scoped.
Any dedicated Finance ledger UI requires explicit client confirmation before implementation.

tasks.story: As the platform owner, I want all important actions logged so the system is traceable.
acceptance: Audit log records action type, actor, affected entity, timestamp, and relevant metadata.

Logged actions include job created, changed, published, reverted to draft, directly assigned, job reassigned, accepted, marked performed, cancelled, Report Problem submitted, special case created, special case resolved, admin cancellation decision, document uploaded/replaced/rejected/accepted, driver invoice upload registered/updated/deleted, PDF generated/regenerated, acceptance confirmation, driver blocked/inactivated/archived/access reset, audit log export, admin alert sent, and feature flag changes if used.
Status history records previous status, new status, date/time, triggering user, optional comment, and uploaded evidence if applicable.
Admin can view audit log entries in the admin console.
Admin can download or export the audit log for offline search, filtering, and archival; export is admin-only and is itself auditable.
Audit log is a traceability surface, not the only urgent notification mechanism.
Every operational status change writes status history with previous status, new status, date and time, triggering user, optional comment, and reference to uploaded evidence when applicable.
Cancellation records cancellationActor as driver, admin/dispatching, or customer with reason code and optional free text.

tasks.story: As a user, I want clear feedback when an action cannot be completed so I understand what happened.
acceptance: If job is already taken, driver sees a clear already-taken message.
If a driver tries to casually return an accepted job, the UI directs them to Report Problem instead.
If blocked or inactive driver opens marketplace active actions, access denied or eligibility message is shown.
If PDF generation fails, admin sees retry/regenerate option and error is logged.
Validation errors in admin forms identify missing or invalid fields.
Date/time validation warnings are clear and can be consciously confirmed by admin where business rules allow.
If document is rejected, driver sees correction needed and rejection reason.
If map navigation cannot open a preferred app, fallback behavior opens a standard map URL or shows copyable address.
If distance estimation fails, admin can enter distance manually and optionally retry calculation.
Foreign or unknown postal codes do not crash filtering or hide jobs incorrectly.

tasks.story: As an admin, I want to export relevant job data so operations and financial reporting can continue outside the system where needed.
acceptance: Admin can export relevant job data and the audit log in CSV or Excel format.
Export includes operational fields such as customer, pickup and delivery locations, time windows, driver, operational status, document status, distance, and basic financial fields where in scope.
Export may include columns needed for driver invoice and document tracking where operations require it.
Optional export column preset maps to legacy spreadsheet columns for continuity, but historical Excel tour import and CSV mass import are out of Version 1.
Export respects admin-only access.
Legacy template files may contain spreadsheet errors; exports are system-generated and validated, not raw re-export of broken templates.
Advanced dashboards/BI are out of scope unless separately approved.

tasks.story: As a driver, I want the PWA to work reliably on mobile devices so I can use it during daily operations.
acceptance: PWA is mobile-first and responsive for smartphones.
Tablet/iPad compatibility target remains an open question for confirmation.
PWA can be added to home screen where supported.
Navigation is fast and optimized for frequent use.
Important actions remain usable on small screens, including accept, Report Problem, upload documents, open external maps, and pull to refresh.
Marketplace and relevant list screens support pull to refresh.
iOS and Android behavior is tested, especially push notification onboarding.
External map app handoff works where supported by device and installed apps.

tasks.story: As a QA engineer, I want automated and manual coverage for critical workflows so the platform is safe to operate.
acceptance: Test: two simultaneous accepts result in exactly one success and one clean failure.
Test: blocked or inactive driver cannot access marketplace active actions or accept jobs.
Test: admin cannot deactivate a driver with assigned, accepted, or special_case tours; activation succeeds when jobs are cleared.
Test: pre-acceptance driver cannot fetch locked fields.
Test: Draft can Publish or Assign (with active driver selection), but Published cannot Assign in Phase 1 without returning to Draft.
Test: admin can reassign Assigned, Accepted, or Special case tour to another active driver; same driver selection is rejected; audit log records reassignment.
Test: admin can revert Published job to Draft; job leaves marketplace and can Publish or Assign again from Draft.
Test: direct assigned jobs do not appear in marketplace.
Test: acceptance unlocks full details, navigation actions, and PDF for accepted driver only.
Test: driver can use Report Problem paths: Cancel Order and Report Order as Not Performable.
Test: Report Order as Not Performable creates Special Case and does not automatically cancel the job.
Test: cancellation records actor, timestamp, reason, optional text, and optional evidence.
Test: admin can resolve Special Case by continuing, adjusting, republishing, closing, or cancelling where rules allow.
Test: admin can cancel Accepted, Assigned, Performed, or Special Case job; job leaves marketplace and active driver lists as appropriate.
Test: operational completion changes status to Performed, not document/settlement Completed.
Test: document statuses are independent from operational job status.
Test: driver invoice/document upload requires job id and Performed status for driver uploads; rejection reason creates visible correction need on Performed My Jobs only.
Test: marketplace pull to refresh fetches current data while full real-time list mutation is not required.
Test: external map navigation actions produce valid handoff URLs or fallback behavior.
Test: admin email alert is generated for critical events if alert module is included.
Test: PDF generation includes required template fields.
Test: audit logs are created for critical actions.
Test: admin can export audit log for download and export creates auditable event.
Test: driver_visibility_matrix enforced — customer and full addresses hidden before acceptance, visible after.
Test: first document upload after Performed sets document_review_summary to Under Review and triggers admin notification.
Test: admin notification feed receives critical events from notification_channels_matrix.
Test: driver in-app notification shows document rejection reason and Infopoint unread badge.
Test: three push toggles independent; direct assign does not send new-job marketplace push by default.

tasks.epic: Tour Documents, Driver Invoices & Billing Reconciliation
tasks.story: As a driver, I want to upload documents per tour; as an admin, I want to review documents and driver invoices with clear statuses, rejection reasons, and optional bulk download.
acceptance: All uploads are linked to a specific valid job/tour; API requires job id on create.
Driver can upload multiple document types, not only invoices.
Document types include invoice, fuel receipt, toll receipt, delivery note, waiting time evidence, other proof, and other receipt.
Upload UI allows category selection through modal, overlay, or equivalent clear interaction.
Accepted file types include PDF, JPG, PNG, and common image formats such as JPEG and WebP unless compliance policy changes.
Production persists files in secure storage with appropriate access control; prototype metadata-only placeholders are not sufficient for production.
Blocked or restricted drivers cannot create uploads.

Admin documents/driver invoices view lists uploads with file metadata, uploader, driver, linked job/tour, upload time, document type, review status, processed flag where applicable, source driver/admin, and actions; review status is shown via compact status pill, not a persistent full-row background highlight.
Admin can view, download, accept, reject, mark checked, mark processed/completed, and add internal notes or rejection reasons depending on document type.
Driver can see whether an uploaded document is accepted, rejected, or under review.
Rejected documents create a visible correction need for the driver and show the rejection reason.
Driver can replace rejected or pending (uploaded) documents on performed tours only; replacement resets review to uploaded.
Correction badge appears only on performed tours in My Jobs, not on active (accepted/assigned) tours.
Admin can download individual invoices/documents.
Admin can select multiple invoices and download selected invoices in bulk if driver invoice module is included.
Optional filtering by document type should be supported or listed as a later improvement if not included in V1.
No direct accounting system integration is required in Version 1; external bookkeeping upload and payment remain manual.
Driver invoices are separated from full Finance dashboard and should not depend on advanced finance analytics.
Changing upload processed flag, link to job, or deletion triggers reconciliation on affected jobs if invoice-received derivation is implemented.
Document and driver invoice module may be offered as base V1 or optional module according to final business decision.
Where supported by device/browser capabilities, upload UX should allow camera capture or scan-like capture so receipts and evidence are clearly legible.

Document type-specific helper notices can be shown, for example reminding drivers to include the vehicle registration number on fuel receipts or to document start, end, and reason for waiting time evidence.
If final legal/template rules require it, driver-facing copy should preserve the invoice submission SLA, for example single or consolidated invoice submission within seven calendar days.
Tour detail provides separate Upload document / receipt action alongside invoice upload in driver UI.
On first tour-linked upload after operational Performed, job document_review_summary automatically becomes Under Review and admin is notified.
Admin can mark document as checked before accept/reject when review workflow requires it.
Example admin rejection reasons include fuel receipt not legible, registration number missing, and waiting time not documented comprehensibly.
Driver sees per-document status accepted, rejected, or under review with rejection reason when rejected.

tasks.story: As an admin and driver, I want distance estimation for order pricing support and external map handoff for driver navigation without building an internal map module.
acceptance: System estimates distance after pickup and delivery addresses are complete.
Distance estimation uses a realistic drivable route over public roads, not straight-line distance.
Where the selected map API supports route alternatives, the shortest drivable route by kilometers is preferred over fastest route.
Admin can manually override calculated distance.
Manual distance override is not overwritten unexpectedly when addresses are edited.
Admin can trigger Recalculate Distance or equivalent when needed.
Calculated distance supports manual price band decisions but does not hard lock pricing.
Map/distance API choice and ongoing usage costs must be confirmed before implementation.
Driver navigation uses external map apps via address handoff/deep links; no internal map module is built in V1.

tasks.story: As the project team, I want a clear rollout approach so the client can move from Excel to the new system without historical migration risk.
acceptance: System rollout starts from zero with new tours created in the system from launch date.
Historical Excel tours are not migrated into the new system.
Selected master data and drivers may be preloaded if useful.
Client may run Excel and the new system in parallel for a short period during transition.
Rollout can begin with tech-friendly drivers first.
Client prepares instructions, guides, and communication for drivers.
Phone and email fallback may remain during transition for operational safety.
CSV mass import is not part of Version 1 and remains future scope unless later requested.

out_of_scope: Chat / messaging between admin and drivers
GPS tracking, live tracking, or real-time driver location
Internal custom map module; Version 1 only hands addresses to external map apps
Full Finance dashboard and advanced finance analytics unless separately confirmed
Advanced BI dashboards and margin automation beyond basic recorded fields
Accounting/invoicing system integration; export/download and manual bookkeeping upload remain in scope where specified
Vehicle condition documentation or protocol app integration, including thousands of images and plausibility checks
Historical Excel tour migration into the new system
CSV mass import for customers, addresses, tours, or other large datasets in Version 1
Native support for complex A to B to A or A to B to C tour chains; these are represented as separate A to B orders for now
Rich newsletter editor, replies, attachments, or advanced formatting for News; Version 1 news is short one-way text
Full backend notification center unless the chosen platform makes it trivial; Version 1 may use admin email alerts
Full product multilingual/i18n rollout and localized legal copy management unless separately scoped; demo locales may exist in prototype only
External customer import interfaces or PDF recognition unless separately scoped
Any behavior not described in PRD v1.5, validated wireframes, client feedback, or approved change request

resolved_defaults.documents_module_production: Optional/costed line item; prototype includes full module enabled.
resolved_defaults.document_rejection_ux: Correction required badge on performed tours in My Jobs only; driver/admin can replace rejected or uploaded files on performed tours; no separate correction queue in V1.
resolved_defaults.map_distance_production: Technical spike required; prefer shortest drivable route by km when API supports route alternatives.
resolved_defaults.driver_auth_production: Keycloak selected for authentication. AUTHEON links app_users to the Keycloak subject; exact realm/client setup and driver login ceremony remain implementation configuration.
resolved_defaults.admin_email_alerts_v1: Global dispatcher mailbox list; per-event configuration deferred.
resolved_defaults.blocked_inactive_history: Inactive and archived: read-only historical tours; blocked: no marketplace actions, read-only history.
resolved_defaults.pdf_generation_timing: On acceptance or direct assignment, not on publish.
resolved_defaults.pdf_tax: Amounts as entered; no automated VAT until legal confirms.
resolved_defaults.vehicle_csv_role: Glossary/reference only; not operational import data.
resolved_defaults.app_display_name: Configurable; default Transport Portal until client provides final name.
resolved_defaults.audit_log_language_v1: English action keys; UI localized EN/DE.
resolved_defaults.driver_master_edits: Admins may edit all fields; audit status changes, access reset, block, archive.
resolved_defaults.camera_and_document_helpers: File picker in V1; per-type helper notices in prototype; camera capture when browser supports capture attribute.
resolved_defaults.report_problem_ui: Primary footer label Report problem / Problem melden with warning-style icon.
resolved_defaults.admin_new_job_entry: Primary CTA on Jobs overview only; no separate New job item in admin sidebar navigation.
resolved_defaults.customer_input_v1: Required select from customer master data; free-text customer field not used for production create form.
resolved_defaults.direct_assign_and_reassign_v1: Assign from Draft and reassign on Assigned/Accepted/Special case require picking an active driver; reassignment preserves status.

special_case_resolution_actions.continue: Resume tour to statusBeforeSpecialCase captured when Report Problem created the special case (assigned or accepted), not always assigned

resolved_defaults.special_case_resolution_label_guidance: Avoid ambiguous Close for administrative close; prefer labels such as Mark performed and close case vs Cancel order.
resolved_defaults.admin_notification_feed_v1: Lightweight in-admin event list for critical events; complements email alerts; not a full notification center.
resolved_defaults.account_provisioning_v1: Prototype-generated temporary passwords are demo-only. In production, public /v1/users self-registration is removed; admins create driver/admin accounts and resend access from the admin UI, but credentials are delivered through Keycloak required-action/invite emails so users set their own passwords. AUTHEON does not expose generated passwords in the UI or store them in the application database.
resolved_defaults.production_roles_v1: Use Keycloak/application roles admin and driver. Driver workflows must resolve the local driver profile from the authenticated Keycloak subject server-side; clients must not submit or spoof driverId for self-actions.
resolved_defaults.backend_schema_strategy_v1: Backend schema changes follow existing TypeORM entity synchronization for now; explicit database migrations are deferred and must be planned before production hardening with DB_SYNCHRONIZE=false.
resolved_defaults.backend_finance_boundary_v1: Only basic job financial fields are implemented and must be feature-gated. Full Finance dashboard and advanced finance analytics remain future scope unless separately approved.
resolved_defaults.backend_notification_routing_v1: Backend notification routing follows notification_channels_matrix. Critical business events are routed to admin feed/email or driver in-app/push as specified; technical account sync and non-critical CRUD events remain audit/outbox records only.
resolved_defaults.document_review_checked_step: Optional admin mark-as-checked before accept/reject when operations require it.
resolved_defaults.client_display_status_labels: UI may show Under review and Completed from document_review_summary and settlement Closed without adding operational statuses.
resolved_defaults.master_data_change_request_v1: Driver request queue; one open per driver; admin Approve & save applies updateDriver; Reject without save; audit master_data_change_requested|approved|rejected; driver in-app notify on resolution.
resolved_defaults.report_problem_cancel_ux_v1: Binding warning + driver terms link before slide; seven cancellation reason codes per Feedback.pdf C.1; warning icon on tour detail and sheet header.
resolved_defaults.report_problem_not_performable_evidence_: Optional file upload on not performable (PDF/images, capped in prototype); listed on admin special-case panel; metadata only in demo.

prototype_assumptions.purpose: Client-facing UX validation for PRD v1.5; in-memory only.
prototype_assumptions.no_backward_compatibility: v1.4 statuses (return_requested, completed) and return-window flows removed entirely.
prototype_assumptions.auth: Persona switcher only; no real magic link.
prototype_assumptions.persistence: In-memory store; page refresh resets demo state.
prototype_assumptions.maps_distance: Mock estimate on address completeness; recalculate button; external navigation via Google Maps search URLs.
prototype_assumptions.admin_email_alerts: Audit log entry plus optional toast; no SMTP.
prototype_assumptions.push_notifications: Audit queue entries only; no service worker.
prototype_assumptions.documents_module: ENABLED by default for full walkthrough.
prototype_assumptions.finance_module: DISABLED; Finance nav removed from admin.
prototype_assumptions.pdf: HTML/text preview from structured job fields; not Word template engine.
prototype_assumptions.files: Metadata and placeholder download only; no binary persistence.
prototype_assumptions.admin_nav: Jobs overview hosts primary New job button; no New job sidebar nav item.
prototype_assumptions.cancel_slide: Slide-to-cancel locked until explanation >= 10 chars with disabled track styling in prototype.
prototype_assumptions.special_case_continue: reportProblemNotPerformable stores statusBeforeSpecialCase on specialCaseReport; Continue restores that status and driverState.acceptedIds when prior was accepted.
prototype_assumptions.master_data_crud: Customers and addresses: full create/read/update/delete in admin UI; inactive flag; delete blocked when tours reference record; new job uses selects; optional save manual address to master on draft save.
prototype_assumptions.schedule_multi_day: Pickup and delivery each have date + window from/to; prototype new job form, job detail, overview, and driver views show both legs; delivery before pickup shows admin warning only.
prototype_assumptions.tour_billing_workspace: Admin nav labeled Tour documents lists all tourDocuments with filters (document type, review status, source) and type column; section id remains invoices in prototype HTML routing.
prototype_assumptions.tour_invoice_document_type: Single canonical code `invoice` for driver billing uploads; legacy billing-invoice codes are normalized on load/write in store.
prototype_assumptions.document_review_statuses_prototype: Per-file review: uploaded, accepted, rejected, correction_required. Admin reject → rejected; Require correction → correction_required (driver badge + job Correction Required). Legacy missing/under_review → uploaded on load.
prototype_assumptions.payment_status_prototype: paymentStatus values: Invoice Missing (default), Invoice Received (auto on invoice accept), Paid (manual Finance only). Unpaid removed; legacy Unpaid normalized to Invoice Missing.
prototype_assumptions.supplier_invoice_number_prototype: Each tour document may store supplierInvoiceNumber and optional supplierInvoiceDate. Admin Accept on invoice type requires supplierInvoiceNumber (modal); acceptTourDocument() is the only path to accepted. job.invoiceNumber copies from the latest accepted invoice document, not from fileName. Replace/upload resets supplier fields until re-accepted.
prototype_assumptions.seed_mock_data: 17 addresses, 5 customers, 3 drivers, 10 jobs (full locations + contacts), 6 tour documents (performed tours only), 2 admin email alerts, 5 audit entries; validateSeedData() runs on load and after reloadDemo().
prototype_assumptions.assign_driver_picker: AssignDriverDialog modal; assignJob/reassignJob by driver id; getAssignableDrivers() returns Active only.
prototype_assumptions.customer_select: New job section 01 uses select populated from customer master data; sets customerId + customer name.
prototype_assumptions.order_entry_formatters_prototype: inputFormatters.js: formatDateInput, formatTimeInput, normalizeVin, normalizePlate, compareTimeStrings; wired on admin new-order blur; MANUFACTURER_SUGGESTIONS datalist on brand field.
prototype_assumptions.master_data_change_requests_prototype: store.masterDataChangeRequests[]; requestMasterDataChange, listMasterDataChangeRequests, getOpenMasterDataChangeRequestForDriver, resolveMasterDataChangeRequest, getOpenMasterDataChangeRequestCount; MasterDataRequestsPane in admin.jsx; metadata-only evidence on specialCaseReport.evidence.

production_open_questions: Confirm Keycloak email action settings for account invites and password resets: sender domain, templates, link expiry, required actions, and whether SMTP is handled by Keycloak or AUTHEON's mail provider.
Confirm which map or distance API vendor to use and acceptable ongoing usage budget.
Confirm production file retention period, encryption at rest, malware scanning, and DSGVO handling for tour document uploads.
Confirm final client-provided app display name string for production UI and legal copy.
Confirm production UI labels for special case administrative close vs cancel order (avoid ambiguous Close).
risks: Legal wording inside the transport-order PDF requires legal review before production.
Atomic marketplace acceptance requires transaction-safe implementation; wrong implementation can cause double assignment.
Replacing flexible Excel workflows with rigid software can block rare edge cases; mitigate with Special Case status, admin override, and manual correction paths.
Automatically saving every private one-time address can bloat address master data; mitigate with optional save control.
Document rejection creates a follow-up loop for drivers; the PWA must make correction needs clearly visible.
Finance dashboard was not validated and may cause scope creep; keep full finance out of base V1 and focus on driver invoices if included.
PWA push behavior differs between Android and iOS; iOS requires careful onboarding and testing.
Admin audit log can bury urgent events; use email alerts for critical admin events in V1 if backend notification center is not trivial.
Map/distance API usage may create ongoing costs and route ambiguity; choose API carefully, cache where appropriate, and preserve manual override.
Vehicle condition documentation can become a separate large project due to photos, storage, and plausibility checks; keep it future scope.
Rollout may be difficult for less tech-friendly drivers; begin with tech-friendly drivers and maintain operational fallback during transition.
If Directus or a similar admin backend is used, critical workflow state changes must still go through controlled business logic to avoid inconsistent data.
Legacy vehicle-list CSV in the repository may contain spreadsheet errors; use only as a column glossary unless cleansed and separately approved for import.
Branding remains unresolved because the client does not want the final UI to use AUTHEON as the program name.

Simple driver push notification settings and postal code prefix preferences
Admin email alerts for critical events
Information Center / Infopoint with general documents and short one-way news
Driver management with blocked/inactive/archive states and access reset
Audit log and status history
PDF generation from structured order data
Rollout from zero with no historical tour migration
Admin permanent delete for Draft jobs only
Admin Jobs overview primary New job entry without duplicate sidebar nav item
Admin lightweight notification feed for critical events
Driver in-app notifications for tour and document events
Explicit driver_visibility_matrix and marketplace_sync_policy
client_status_mapping for Under review and Completed display labels
Driver profile change request workflow with admin queue and one-open-request rule
Admin inline driver master-data edit on approve from change-request detail (production may use same fields as Users module)

Driver invoice review with bulk download
Keycloak realm/client configuration and exact driver login ceremony
